def find_all(text, pattern):
    n = len(text)
    m = len(pattern)
    if m == 0:
        return []
    if m > n:
        return []
    # Encode to bytes for fast byte-level comparison
    text_b = text.encode('utf-8') if isinstance(text, str) else text
    pat_b = pattern.encode('utf-8') if isinstance(pattern, str) else pattern
    nb = len(text_b)
    mb = len(pat_b)
    # Build optimized KMP failure table
    fail = [0] * mb
    k = 0
    for i in range(1, mb):
        while k > 0 and pat_b[k] != pat_b[i]:
            k = fail[k - 1]
        if pat_b[k] == pat_b[i]:
            k += 1
        fail[i] = k
    # Two-stage search: use first character pre-check for early skip
    first_char = pat_b[0]
    results = []
    results_append = results.append
    j = 0
    for i in range(nb):
        c = text_b[i]
        while j > 0 and pat_b[j] != c:
            j = fail[j - 1]
        if pat_b[j] == c:
            j += 1
        if j == mb:
            results_append(i - mb + 1)
            j = fail[j - 1]
    return results

def find_all(text, pattern):
    n = len(text)
    m = len(pattern)
    if m == 0:
        return []
    if m > n:
        return []
    # Convert strings to bytes for fast indexing
    text_b = text.encode('latin-1') if isinstance(text, str) else text
    pat_b = pattern.encode('latin-1') if isinstance(pattern, str) else pattern
    nb = len(text_b)
    mb = len(pat_b)
    if mb == 1:
        # Special case: single character search
        target = pat_b[0]
        return [i for i in range(nb) if text_b[i] == target]
    # Build KMP failure table
    fail = [0] * mb
    k = 0
    for i in range(1, mb):
        while k > 0 and pat_b[k] != pat_b[i]:
            k = fail[k - 1]
        if pat_b[k] == pat_b[i]:
            k += 1
        fail[i] = k
    # KMP search with local variable caching
    results = []
    append = results.append
    j = 0
    for i in range(nb):
        c = text_b[i]
        while j > 0 and pat_b[j] != c:
            j = fail[j - 1]
        if pat_b[j] == c:
            j += 1
        if j == mb:
            append(i - mb + 1)
            j = fail[j - 1]
    return results
